modernization.description: Integration of Bayraktar TB3 carrier-capable drones as primary fixed-wing aviation component
modernization.impact: Transforms from traditional amphibious ship to drone carrier hybrid

modernization.description: Continued replacement of foreign systems with Turkish alternatives including ASELSAN radars and communication systems
modernization.impact: Reduces foreign dependency and aligns with Turkish defense industry goals

vulnerabilities.description: Lacks organic fighter aircraft for air superiority, relies on point defense systems and escorts
vulnerabilities.context: Operating in contested airspace without F-35B capability severely limits operational flexibility
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Drone swarm tactics, escort coordination, and potential future STOVL fighter development

vulnerabilities.description: TB3 carrier operations represent unproven concept in naval aviation
vulnerabilities.context: No real-world experience with large-scale drone carrier operations in combat
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Extensive testing and training programs, gradual capability buildup

vulnerabilities.description: Turkey's entire amphibious assault capability concentrated in one ship
vulnerabilities.context: Loss or damage to Anadolu would eliminate Turkish expeditionary capability
vulnerabilities.mitigation: No current mitigation, though second ship has been discussed

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Regional power projection, amphibious exercises, diplomatic missions
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Escorted by Turkish frigates, supported by submarine
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Still building operational experience, crew training ongoing

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Anadolu features Turkish systems integration and drone optimization vs Spanish F-35B/Harrier configuration. Similar size but different air wing philosophy.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Original vs modified: How Turkey transformed a proven design for drone warfare

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Trieste operates F-35B fighters and focuses on traditional naval aviation, while Anadolu pioneers drone operations. Similar amphibious capability but different air power approach.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Manned vs unmanned: Two philosophies of modern amphibious warfare

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Izumo being modified for F-35B operations while Anadolu pursues drone-centric model. Both represent regional power projection aspirations but different technical solutions.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Rising regional powers: Japan's traditional approach vs Turkey's drone revolution

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Queen Elizabeth is larger supercarrier with F-35B focus, while Anadolu is smaller amphibious ship with drone emphasis. Different scale and mission focus.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Size vs innovation: Can drone swarms challenge traditional carrier aviation?

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 075 emphasizes helicopter operations and traditional amphibious assault, while Anadolu focuses on fixed-wing drone operations. Similar strategic intent, different execution.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: East vs West amphibious warfare: China's helicopter model vs Turkey's drone approach
